task: I want to put together a really easy beginner sketch reference pack for a casual drawing session, and I’d like you to grab the pieces in the browser so I can look at them afterward. First, on Pinterest, find one pin with a genuinely useful person drawing reference photo for figure practice—something clear enough that a beginner could sketch from—and open the actual pin page, then keep that tab open and save the pin link for me. Once you’ve got that figure reference, go to Google Images and search for a basic one-room hut from the 1500s in an English style so I can use it as a simple background setting; open the image result or source page that looks most believable and leave that open too so I can see the picture itself. After that, go to Bored Panda and open their collection of angry cat photos as a fun mood reference for the character expression, and tell me the exact page title while keeping that page open as well. In the end, send me the Pinterest pin link, the hut image or source page link, and the Bored Panda page title, plus a short note on how the figure, the hut, and the angry-cat mood could all work together in one simple sketch idea.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to a Pinterest pin page (https://www.pinterest.com/pin/627759635542870439/) that features a clear, full-body stock photo of a person standing, which is suitable for figure practice. The agent kept this tab open and provided the link in the final response.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for and opened a source page (naturalhomes.org/timeline/wherwell2.htm) that features a clear, high-quality image of a 500-year-old oak-frame, wattle-and-daub thatched cottage in Wherwell, England. This is a perfect match for the "basic one-room hut from the 1500s in an English style" requirement. The screenshot confirms the visual content of the page.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Bored Panda collection of angry cat photos. In step 26, the snapshot of the page title is explicitly listed as "40 Times People Just Had To Snap A Pic Of Their Angry, But Cute Cats | Bored Panda". The agent correctly reported this title in its final response.

R4: success - The agent provided a final note in step 28 that explicitly references the Pinterest figure reference (a standing pose), the hut reference (the 1500s English cottage), and the Bored Panda mood inspiration (the angry cat expression). It combines these into a coherent, beginner-friendly sketch concept (a character standing in front of the cottage with an annoyed expression because of a cat). This satisfies the requirement.
